matching_rule: One base case matches when a promoter or gene-body variant is visible in the current sample. Three additional case families match when the whitelist mean beta, the gene-name-related mean beta, or the all-numeric mean beta resolves to low, medium, or high methylation. Variant prediction rules receive the decoded sample GT, zygosity, ALT dosage, and site REF -> ALT definition; curated allele rules take precedence only when the sample genotype carries the relevant allele dosage.
disclaimer: Predictive theses in this database are literature-guided research summaries derived from the bundled gene interpretation bundle. They are designed for exploratory synthesis in the UI and should not be treated as diagnostic or therapeutic claims.
seeded_markers: PDE6H human-senses variation model (PDE6H human sensory biology pathway model)
PDE6H regulatory expression context model (PDE6H expression and methylation-context model)
concrete_variant_prediction: The variant observed in this sample suggests a PDE6H human sensory biology thesis: the individual may carry context relevant to visual phototransduction, auditory and vestibular hair-cell mechanics, olfactory and gustatory chemosensation, somatosensory ion channels, pain, itch, temperature, proprioception, and interoceptive sensory signaling. Treat the result as candidate pathway evidence until exact allele, zygosity, phenotype, and external classification are reviewed.

variant_prediction_rules.common_name: PDE6H human sensory biology pathway model
variant_prediction_rules.prediction: GT-confirmed PDE6H human-senses variation model dosage suggests this sample carries the PDE6H prediction context described by the gene-level thesis. The variant observed in this sample suggests a PDE6H human sensory biology thesis: the individual may carry context relevant to visual phototransduction, auditory and vestibular hair-cell mechanics, olfactory and gustatory chemosensation, somatosensory ion channels, pain, itch, temperature, proprioception, and interoceptive sensory signaling. Treat the result as candidate pathway evidence until exact allele, zygosity, phenotype, and external classification are reviewed. This specific marker is curated as PDE6H human sensory biology pathway model. PDE6H human-senses variation model is curated as a PDE6H marker for Human senses, sensory transduction, and sensory-neuron research context, Vision, hearing, balance, smell, taste, touch, pain, itch, temperature, proprioception, and interoception pathway biology. Read it through genotype dosage, phenotype context, and the visual phototransduction, auditory and vestibular hair-cell mechanics, olfactory and gustatory chemosensation, somatosensory ion channels, pain, itch, temperature, proprioception, and interoceptive sensory signaling pathway rather than as a stand-alone deterministic result.
variant_prediction_rules.sample_change_template: This sample matched PDE6H human-senses variation model at site definition {change} with GT {gt_raw} decoded as {genotype} ({zygosity}). Anchor the PDE6H prediction to genotype dosage {allele_dosage} rather than REF -> ALT presence alone. Dosage-specific conclusions should be softened when GT, depth, balance, or genotype quality are uncertain.
variant_prediction_rules.basis: Can modify or tag visual phototransduction, auditory and vestibular hair-cell mechanics, olfactory and gustatory chemosensation, somatosensory ion channels, pain, itch, temperature, proprioception, and interoceptive sensory signaling.
